# Token Saver Desktop

> Local-first desktop software for diagnosing AI-agent token waste and measuring cost per successful task.

Canonical repository: https://github.com/SiruGao/token-saver

## Current status

Token Saver Desktop V1 is a working read-only analyzer built with Tauri 2 and TypeScript.

V1 includes a Dashboard, Doctor, Sessions, Integrations, Settings, transcript import, local agent detection, read-only session scanning, usage normalization, token estimation, local report export, and six deterministic waste rules.

Supported detection targets include Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, OpenClaw, Hermes Agent, OpenCode, and Cursor.

The OpenClaw SKILL.md remains an optional integration and is not the product core.

V1 does not yet modify prompts, apply automatic compression, claim verified savings, or provide signed public installers.

## Product direction

- V1: Desktop Analyzer and Doctor.
- V1.1: agent-specific parsers and SQLite Proof Ledger.
- V1.2: exact deduplication, cache alignment, and reversible compaction.
- V2: local Gateway, Quality Guard, replay, provider reconciliation, and verified cost-per-success reporting.

Primary metric: cost per successful task.

- Contributing: https://github.com/SiruGao/token-saver/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

## Core principles

- Measure before optimizing.
- Prefer exact elimination, caching, delta context, and retrieval over lossy summaries.
- Use provider-reported usage as billing truth.
- Include retries and rework in cost calculations.
- Keep processing local by default.
- Keep V1 read-only.
- Publish reproducible evidence for headline claims.

## Search concepts

AI agent desktop app, AI agent token optimization, LLM cost optimization, context engineering, prompt caching, token usage dashboard, AI FinOps, token observability, MCP optimization, local-first AI, provider usage reconciliation, cost per successful task.
